errors: [Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 18 but ELM uses age >= 25. Time interval mismatch: CPG requires 12-month lookback for encounters but ELM uses 6 months.]
[Age range upper limit mismatch: CPG specifies 85 years but ELM implementation uses 80 years]
[HbA1c threshold value mismatch: CPG specifies 6.5% but ELM implementation uses 9 g% for HbA1c Laboratory Test criteria]

[The ELM implementation does not include the 20 pack-year smoking history requirement or the 15-year quit time frame for former smokers as specified in the CPG. The "Has Smoking History" logic only checks for active tobacco use status via the value set, but lacks explicit pack-year and quit time criteria. The CPG requires both pack-years and quit time for former smokers, which are missing in the ELM implementation.]
[Age thresholds in ELM implementation are incorrect (60-75 years) compared to CPG requirements (45-75 years). Time interval for recent screening in ELM is 2 years vs 1 year required by CPG.]
[1. Time interval for recent anxiety screening is 6 months in ELM, but CPG requires 1 year. 2. Rationale in ELM is always null, but CPG specifies a non-null rationale when InPopulation = true.]
[BMI threshold in ELM is 35 kg/m2 instead of 25 kg/m2 as per CPG; time interval for recent glucose test in ELM is 1 year instead of 3 years as per CPG]

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation do not match CPG requirements. CPG specifies age 21-65, but ELM's "In Demographic" checks age >=30. The ELM incorrectly excludes women aged 21-29 from the target population.]

The ELM implementation incorrectly uses a 3-month lookback period for recent depression screening (in "Has Recent Adult Depression Screening" and "Has Recent Adolescent Depression Screening") instead of the 1-year lookback period specified in the CPG requirements. This mismatch in time intervals (3 months vs. 1 year) directly conflicts with the CPG's exclusion criteria for recent screening. All other value sets, logical operators, and age thresholds are correctly implemented.

[Age threshold in ELM implementation is 75 years (In Demographic: age >=75), but CPG requires age >=65 years. This creates a mismatch in inclusion criteria. The time interval (1 year) and value set reference (2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028) are correctly implemented.]

[Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >=18 years for inclusion, but ELM implementation uses age >=25 years in "In Demographic" logic. This creates a discrepancy in the target population criteria.]

The ELM implementation incorrectly specifies a 6-month lookback period for DEXA scans instead of the 2-year requirement stated in the CPG. The CPG requires excluding patients with a DEXA scan performed within the past 2 years, but the ELM uses a 6-month interval. This directly contradicts the CPG's "No DEXA scan in past 2 years" criterion.

[Age threshold in ELM implementation is incorrect. CPG requires age >=65 years, but ELM defines "In Demographic" as age >=55 years. The upper age limit of 75 matches correctly. This discrepancy in lower age bound (55 vs 65) creates a mismatch in inclusion criteria.]

[The logical structure in the ELM implementation incorrectly uses an OR operator between "Screening Age" and "Gender" in the MeetsInclusionCriteria calculation. The CPG requires ALL inclusion criteria (age >=40 AND female sex) to be true, but the ELM uses (Screening Age OR Gender) AND Mammogram. This would incorrectly include patients who are either over 40 OR female (but not necessarily both), violating the CPG requirements.]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly defines "InPopulation" as only requiring [In Demographic] (age >=18). The CPG requires "InPopulation" to be true only when both age >=18 AND no recent tobacco screening (within 1 year) exists. The ELM is missing the critical "AND NOT Has Recent Tobacco Screening" condition in the InPopulation logic.]

The ELM implementation is missing the exclusion check for active Bipolar Disorder in the "Needs Depression Screening" logic. The CPG requires checking for both active Bipolar Disorder and active Depression Diagnosis as exclusion criteria, but the ELM only excludes active Depression Diagnosis. The Bipolar Diagnosis check is present in the ELM's "Has Bipolar Diagnosis" definition but is not incorporated into the final "Needs Depression Screening" calculation. This creates a logical mismatch where patients with active Bipolar Disorder would not be correctly excluded from screening.

The logical structure for MeetsInclusionCriteria is incorrect. The CPG requires age >=50 AND **no** colonoscopy in the past 1 month to meet inclusion criteria. However, the ELM incorrectly uses `Required Age AND Colonoscopy` (where `Colonoscopy` is true if a colonoscopy exists). This inverts the logic: the ELM would recommend screening for patients with a recent colonoscopy, which contradicts the CPG. The correct logic should be `Required Age AND NOT Colonoscopy`.

[The ELM implementation incorrectly combines inclusion and exclusion criteria. The "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" logic uses "AND" between In Demographic and Has Recent Falls Assessment, but the CPG requires "AND NOT" to implement the exclusion criteria. The correct logic should be: In Demographic AND NOT Has Recent Falls Assessment. This logical inversion would cause the system to recommend screening when a recent assessment exists (contrary to CPG requirements).]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly combines the exclusion criteria for Bipolar and Depression Diagnoses using an AND operator within the NOT clause. The CPG requires exclusion if either diagnosis is present (i.e., NOT (Has Bipolar Diagnosis) AND NOT (Has Depression Diagnosis)), but the ELM uses NOT (Has Bipolar Diagnosis AND Has Depression Diagnosis), which would incorrectly include patients with either diagnosis unless both are present.]

The ELM implementation incorrectly uses an AND operator for exclusion criteria (Connective Tissue Disorders AND Amiodarones) instead of the required OR operator as specified in the CPG. The exclusion logic should trigger if **either** condition is true, not both. This logical mismatch would incorrectly include patients who meet one exclusion criterion but not the other.

The value set reference for "Alcohol Use Screening" in the ELM implementation (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278) does not match the CPG-specified value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542). All other numeric values (age threshold of 18 years, 1-year lookback period) and logical structure (AND operators, exclusion criteria) align with the CPG requirements.
